notes: Correct behavior: Mend reports three direct deps — 'my-lodash', 'old-lodash', 'hono'. The alias names are the install-time identities. 'lodash' should NOT appear as a package name.
Correct behavior: 'my-lodash' and 'old-lodash' are two distinct tree entries even though both resolve to the upstream package 'lodash'. They differ in alias name AND in resolved version.

Failure mode A (alias name collapsed): Mend strips the bun.lock tuple key and uses the first tuple element's package name ('lodash'). Result: tree shows 'lodash' instead of 'my-lodash'/'old-lodash'. Identity lost.
Failure mode B (merge to single entry): Two aliases to 'lodash' are collapsed into one 'lodash' node. Result: only 2 direct deps instead of 3, or a single 'lodash' entry that conflates both versions.

Failure mode C (version from wrong field): Mend reads the version from package.json specifier ('npm:lodash@^4.17.0') rather than the resolved tuple ('lodash@4.17.21'). Result: wrong version or unresolvable semver range reported.

Failure mode D (zero detection): Mend cannot parse Bun's JSONC lockfile format (tuple-based packages section). All packages dropped. Bun is not a named UA resolver — npm-resolver fallback may not understand Bun's tuple format.

warnings: Bun is NOT a UA-supported resolver (js-bun is absent from the Mend install-tool list). Detection falls back to the npm resolver reading bun.lock. The npm resolver was designed for package-lock.json, whose packages section uses object-per-package rather than Bun's tuple format.

ALIAS IDENTITY CONTRACT: The dep tree MUST report aliases by their ALIAS NAME ('my-lodash', 'old-lodash'), NOT by the upstream package name ('lodash'). The alias name is the install-time identity — it is what lives in node_modules/ and what dependents import.

TWO DISTINCT ENTRIES CONTRACT: 'my-lodash@4.17.21' and 'old-lodash@3.10.1' are two separate direct dependencies. They must appear as two separate nodes in the dep tree. Collapsing them into a single 'lodash' node is incorrect and loses the version distinction.

VERSION CONTRACT: The resolved version for each alias entry must come from the tuple ('lodash@4.17.21'), not from the semver specifier in package.json ('^4.17.0'). A parser that extracts the range instead of the resolved version will produce incorrect output.
HONO CONTROL: 'hono@4.7.11' is a plain (non-aliased) registry dep included as a control. If Mend misdetects it, the aliasing logic is not the only broken path.
